summary: Importing projects from godot mobile to desktop does not work

description.text:
**Your Godot version:**
**4.4.1.stable(mobile)** and **4.4.1.stable.official(windows)**

**Issue description:**
So basically, I was using Godot on Mobile, and I decided to export it to PC so I could switch between editing on both platforms. I took one of my game projects from the mobile version of Godot, moved the entire project folder to my PC, and tried to open it using the desktop editor.

When I did that, an error popped up asking if I wanted to open the project in safe mode—but even then, it wouldn't work. It just tried to open and then crashed. Godot would freeze and close immediately.

I tried a bunch of things to fix it:

- Deleted the .godot folder
- Removed editor config files
- Deleted .import files and .uid identifiers

Nothing worked.

Then I looked into the project logs and saw an error message saying that the Vulkan renderer wasn't supported. From what I know, Vulkan is usually used on mobile or requires specific GPU support. I assumed the issue had to do with the rendering settings.

So I went back to the mobile version of the project, changed the renderer setting to Compatibility, and then imported it again into the desktop version of Godot. This time, it worked.

Here are the logs.

I forgot to mention: the project didn't open at all, neither in normal mode nor in safe mode.

summary: Add keypad codes to the keysym unicode map.

description.text:
## Issue
Linux X11 users are unable to type '=' using a keypad. The key is registered but the unicode character does not show up in text input anywhere in the application. Fixes issue #74578.

## Cause
The function `KeyMappingX11::get_unicode_from_keysym` relies on a HashMap, `xkeysym_unicode_map`, to get unicode code points from the keysym code. However, this map does not include mappings from keypad keysyms.

## Solution
Add the missing keypad keysym mappings (found in `X11/keysymdef.h` on lines 280-316)  to `xkeysym_unicode_map`.

### Testing
This solution was tested successfully on the 4.4.1-stable release build.

summary: Ambiguous tooltips on animation snapping toggles

description.text:
### Tested versions

Present in v4.4.1.stable.nixpkgs [49a5bc7b6], but also in latest `master` (at the time of writing).

### System information

n/a

### Issue description

As I was trying to translate some Godot UI strings into Dutch, I realized that the snapping settings at the bottom of the Animation track editor have very ambiguous tooltips. The current tooltips are as follows:

1. Apply snapping to timeline cursor.
2. Apply snapping to selected key(s).
3. Apply snapping to the nearest integer FPS.

This doesn't make clear what is snapping to what, or how the toggles relate to each other; for example, does the first option snap something else *to* the timeline cursor? Or does it *make* the timeline cursor snap to something else?

After digging through the code for a bit, as far as I can tell, it should be something more like this:

1. Snap timeline cursor to nearest frame.
2. Snap new keys to nearest frame.
3. Round frame time to nearest integer FPS.

(The second one is changed a lot in particular; the current text speaks of "selected key(s)" but all code that actually evaluated this setting seemed to be about *creating* keys, whether from scratch or through duplication, rather than anything to do with selections.)

But I'm not 100% sure that this is a correct understanding of how these settings work, and so I decided to create an issue rather than a PR for now. Does this sound about right?

### Steps to reproduce

n/a

summary: Make bottom panel into available dock slot

description.text:
Supersedes #106426

Adds a `DOCK_SLOT_BOTTOM`, which corresponds to bottom panel. All bottom panel controls are now docks. For compatibility, the old bottom editors are just wrapped in EditorDock.

This mostly brings changes to the dock popup, but also allows dragging docks between sides and bottom (currently only FileSystem):

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7edcc165-777b-45c0-a003-64faecb63961

Other than that, the current dock slot is now properly highlighted, slots unavailable for the current dock are darkened. The "Move to Bottom" button is replaced with a bottom dock slot.

I also added 3 new EditorDock properties: `global` (whether it appears in dock menu and can be closed), `hidden` (whether its tab is hidden) and `title_color`. All old bottom docks are non-global.

Dock's layout is now properly respected, i.e. you can make a dock that is horizontal only (in #106503 the `LAYOUT_VERTICAL` flag was effectively unused). I also added a `LAYOUT_FLOATING` flag, which determines whether the dock can float (all legacy bottom docks can't float).

In follow-ups I plan to update legacy docks to the new system, which would allow stuff like vertical ShaderEditor (it's already possible, but the editor is not adjusted for that, so the option is blocked). I will probably open a tracker for that.

A side-effect of these changes is that bottom docks can be rearranged, but I think it's not really a problem 🤔

Also for the future, I think EditorDockManager can be refactored, as it does too much. Some of its code can be moved to EditorDock and some of its code can be delegated to a new EditorDockContainer class extending TabContainer (the bottom panel would inherit it too). Also the main screen could potentially become a dock slot.

summary: AnimationPlayer > StateMachine > Transitions with SwitchMode Immediate are ignored if current animation has another transition with SwitchMode AtEnd

description.text: ### Tested versions tested in v4.3.stable and v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] ### System information Godot v4.4.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+ and Compatibility) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7602) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads) ### Issue description When two transitions from a single animation in an animation StateMachine are available but only one of them has SwitchMode set to AtEnd, the animationPlayer will sometimes wait for the animation to finish playing and take that transition even though the other transition is available right now. Whether it chooses to do this or not seems to be influenced by what order the transition were made in. For example; in this screenshot new_animation is playing until the end because the transition to new_animation 2 was made more recently and has the SwitchMode set to AtEnd. The animtree is ignoring the transition to RESET even though its SwitchMode is immediate. ### Steps to reproduce Create an animation state machine structured like so: None of these transitions have any requirements. The animation in the middle is a 5 second animation, the others are just the RESET state. When the animation is playing it will rapidly switch back and forth between the middle node and one of the side nodes. #### The Problem If you select the transition it's using to rapidly switch from that side node to the middle node and set the SwitchMode to AtEnd, the state machine will wait until the animation finishes playing to switch to that same side node, even though the other transition is available right now with SwitchMode immediate. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [reproductionproject.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21240911/reproductionproject.zip) ### Workaround Make sure any transition from an animation that have SwitchMode set to AtEnd are made FIRST, and them make any transitions that have SwitchMode set to Immediate afterward.

summary: Inhibiting screensaver does not work in Flatpak sandbox

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.4.1.stable.flathub [49a5bc7b6] and earlier versions

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.stable (49a5bc7b6) - Freedesktop SDK 24.08 (Flatpak runtime) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Mesa Intel(R) UHD Graphics 620 (WHL GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8565U CPU @ 1.80GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Godot's linuxbsd build supports the [freedesktop.org Idle Inhibition Service](https://specifications.freedesktop.org/idle-inhibit-spec/latest/) specification to prevent the desktop session becoming idle & entering the screensaver while a game is running. This API involves calling methods on the `org.freedesktop.ScreenSaver` service on the D-Bus session bus.

Unfortunately this service is not accessible to applications running in a Flatpak sandbox. This applies both to launching a game from the Flathub build of the Godot editor, and to games which are published in Flatpak format. (I would guess that the same applies to Snap but I do not know for sure.)

The XDG Desktop Portal provides a somewhat-similar [Inhibit API](https://flatpak.github.io/xdg-desktop-portal/docs/doc-org.freedesktop.portal.Inhibit.html). It is slightly more general in ways that are not interesting to Godot (a running game should not inhibit logout, user-switching or manually suspending the system, only the session becoming idle), and requires a window ID to tie the request to (similar to the File Chooser portal which Godot already supports). Unfortunately, there is probably a need to support both – while portal APIs can be used by non-sandboxed applications, there are almost certainly systems in the wild which do not have an XDG portal installed but which do support the freedesktop.org API.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install Godot from Flathub
2. Create an empty scene
3. Run it
4. Without touching the keyboard and mouse (but optionally while using a gamepad), wait a few minutes (depending on your desktop settings):

Expected results:

- The screen does not dim
- The screen lock + screensaver does not activate

Actual results (at least on GNOME 43):

- After a few minutes, the screen dims
- If you wait longer, the screensaver engages

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A - reproducible by running an empty scene.

Open Godot 4.4.1 or 4.5 beta 3 on Android.

Open two or more scenes (e.g., SceneA.tscn and SceneB.tscn).

Tap the tab to switch from one scene to another.

summary: Global menu item with single-key accelerator can't be activated

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.5.beta3.official [4d1f26e1f], v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6]

### System information

macOS Sequoia (15.5) - Godot Engine v4.5.beta3.official - Metal (Forward+) - Apple M1 Max (Apple7)

### Issue description

Global menu item with accelerator set to a single key can't be activated via key press.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a menu item and set its accelerator to a single key (i.e. `KEY_A`).

Clicking the menu item sets ColorRect in MRP to red as expected, but pressing the key does not.

```gdscript
var main_menu: RID = NativeMenu.get_system_menu(NativeMenu.MAIN_MENU_ID)
var sub_menu : RID = NativeMenu.create_menu()
NativeMenu.add_item(sub_menu, "menu item", set_color, set_color, null, KEY_A)
NativeMenu.add_submenu_item(main_menu, "Custom", sub_menu)
```

summary: Visual Shader State Persistence - Type Fixes

description.text: Great work @Geometror on improving the Visual Shader Editor's state persistence - this [PR](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/666d7c030bb0d898f607df8c62f8f2bc6282ffd6) did introduce two minor issues that this PR fixes. **Issue 1:** Creating a Visual Shader of type Particle, Sky or Fog creates a broken Output node with no values. **Steps to recreate:** New project, create a new Sky Visual Shader, view in Visual Shader Editor. **Issue 2:** Loading a Particle Visual Shader can fail to restore state and throw an 'Out of Bounds' error in the console. **Steps to recreate:** New Project, create a new Particle Visual Shader, view in Editor and swap type to "Process". Close project, reopen project. **Cause:** ``` int saved_type = vs_editor_cache->get_value(id_string, "edited_type", 0); edit_type->select(saved_type); set_current_shader_type((VisualShader::Type)saved_type); ``` Using 0 as the default value for 'Saved Type' causes the output nodes to display incorrectly for Sky/Fog/Particle shaders because 0 represents a type for Spatial or Canvas shaders only. Because this value is then immediately saved to the config, and may not be able to be changed by the user, this breaks Visual Sky & Fog shaders. Using Saved_Type (which should be an integer representing a value of the Types enum from Visual_Shader.h) for edit_type->select (which expects an index representing an options button) can cause an out of bounds selection, and restore an incorrect state. The code in this PR adds back extra logic to set defaults correctly, and pass appropriate values to the two functions.

summary: Fix MultiSelectEdit to be compatible with the groups editor in the Node Dock

description.text:
MultiSelectEdit is now compatible with the groups editor in the Node Dock. As in https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/10331

Design:
I originally wanted to do a polymorphic design: Implement the same functions on MultiSelectEdit that the groups editor uses to manipulate Nodes. However, the two classes are different enough that this didn't quite work. The final implementation of the groups editor basically asks what type the current selection is, and acts accordingly to add/remove it to a group.

Other changes include changing the editor to properly send selections of both single nodes and MultiSelectEdit objects to the NodeDock, and changing the NodeDock to properly pipe this to the groups editor.

Minimum reproduction:
Create a new scene and add a few nodes. You can verify the first bug by selecting the signal panel and then selecting multiple nodes. All the signals should disappear as the current object of the signal editor becomes null.

Things you can test:
1. The Signal panel will appear empty upon selecting multiple nodes
2. When no nodes are selected, the NodeDock will display a message similar to the current one: "Select a node to edit its signals and groups"
3. When multiple nodes are selected, the groups editor will display them as "in" a group if any one of the nodes is in said group, and out otherwise.
  a. In the former case, double check that removing the nodes from the group, then undoing, only re-adds the portion of your selection that was originally in the group. So, if you select two nodes: Node and Node2, where one is in a group G, you can remove them from G, then undo to return to the original state.
  b. In the latter case, select any set of nodes entirely not in a group, see that you can add and remove it to the group as you please, via the checkbox and the undo/redo stack
4. Test that these changes all maintain the scene in a valid state. After, or halfway through any operation you can save the scene, close and reopen the editor with no issues.

Known bugs:
1. This breaks the "Select a single node to edit its signals and groups" message inherently. Though it still exists in the code, it belongs to the node dock, and making it appear would require some complex, easy to break interactions with the current editor selected in the NodeDock (signals or groups) that I elected not to implement until this got reviewed.
2. Undoing/redoing a multiselect group will be listed as a "Scene Undo" or "Scene Redo" in the editor instead of just printing the message itself. I'm not familiar enough with the UndoRedoManager to know why this is happening.
3. Scene groups do not appear in the groups editor until a node in the group is selected, and then another selection is made. This is an issue with erroneous invalidation of the scene group cache in the groups editor (`GroupsEditor::_update_scene_groups`). But the scene tree being passed to it in this case appears to still be valid, so I'm not sure where it could be coming from.

Wait, why didn't you do signals?
Signals are much bigger, goofier objects than groups and I feel under-qualified. There are more design decisions to consider in how the signal editor interface and signal creator dialog should interact with multiple nodes, and I've spent more time than I planned on this issue already. Thinking about these design choices also reveals that multi select signal editing might not be as useful as the same functionality for groups. Since signals break in the editor much more easily than groups, this sort of behavior would be much more cleanly handled at runtime using scripts. However, it should be much easier to do them in the future if these changes are accepted, as they at least expose MultiNodeEdit to the NodeDock, and if there is enough demand for the feature.

Things that could be improved:
I'm open for suggestions on how to improve this pr. This is my first time contributing to the engine and it took quite a while to get used to even this small corner of the code. I'm not sure if it's worth the time, but in my opinion the sketchiest parts of this implementation in multi_node_edit.cpp's new add_partial_to_group and remove_partial_from_group:
 1. The masking behavior required for selections partially in a group to be undone correctly is done using a String, since that was the easiest thing to pass to the method through the undo/redo manager for me. I really don't know what types are ok here. C++ bitmask would be the appropriate type for the job if I could have things my way but its not implemented as a function argument for methods on the undo redo stack. I also tried Array<bool> but couldn't get it working.
 2. The masking behavior relies on the mask being in the same order as the nodes in the selection. This isn't a problem on its own, but it leads to using boomer loops.
 3. The mask for remove_partial_from_group will technically never be utilized because you can't add a selection to a group it is already partially in. Though the fact that its there would make this easy to change.

Also, I don't know how to test if my changes compile on other systems.

### Issue description

Enabling SDFGI in a scene with objects with strong emissive materials results in permanent, non-updating light spill artifacts on nearby objects in the scene. Only noticeable when you move the emissive object over time.

It looks kind've like it was baked into the scene, but no (intentional) baking has occurred, and as soon as you turn SDFGI off it goes away.

### Steps to reproduce

Create a fresh Godot 4.4.1 project. 
Create a 3d scene.
Add a WorldEnvironment node.
Add a MeshInstance3d floor plane.
Set size to 10 x 10m
Add a Camera3D node, move and face it to show the whole floor plane.
Add a DirectionalLight3d and rotate it x=-15
Set the lights Energy to 0.4.
Set worldenvironment background to custom colour and leave it black.
Add a meshinstance3d box mesh, move it to Y=0.5 and rotate it to -30 to assist showing reflection of emissive object.
Add a meshinstance3d spheremesh.

Under the sphere MeshInstance, add a StandardMaterial3d.
Set Albedo to black.
Set Emission to ON, colour to ff3c00, energymultiplier to 15.
In WorldEnvironment enable SDFGI, leave defaults.
Create a new script on the Sphere mesh, leave defaults
Under the _process function, add this code - position.x = -2. 
Run the project. This moves the object but does not reveal the issue.
Under _process, remove previous code and add this -
	position.x = cos(rot) * 4.0
	position.z = sin(rot) * 4.0
	rot += get_process_delta_time() * 3
Create the rot variable outside of any function - var rot = 0.0
Run the project. The light artifacting should be shown under the default location of the sphere.
Whilst running the project, toggle the SDFGI tick box on and off. SDFGI is recalculated incorrectly.

summary: Add video importer

description.text:
Closes godotengine/godot-proposals#7062.

The goal for this PR is being able to import video files from a variety of formats to be used with the built-in VideoStreamPlayer. The imported video files are converted internally to Ogg Theora (OGV) using an external FFmpeg binary so that users don't have to do it manually.

The user has to set  `filesystem/import/video/ffmpeg_path` in the editor settings to the FFmpeg binary in their system.

The accepted video file extensions are:

- mp4
- webm
- mpg
- mpeg
- mkv
- avi
- mov
- wmv

I've chosen these extensions because they're well known. Suggestions welcomed. The supported video codecs will be the ones compatible with these containers and built into the FFmpeg version used.

The import options are:

- Video quality.
- Audio quality.
- Keyframe interval (GOP).
- Scale width.
- Scale height.
- Alpha channel replacement color. (Replaces the alpha channel, if there's one, with a color.)
- Encoding speed.
- Custom FFmpeg arguments.

~~I would like to add the Theora encoding speed level to allow for faster encoding, but it's not an option in FFmpeg. [I've sent a patch to add this option to FFmpeg](https://ffmpeg.org/pipermail/ffmpeg-devel/2025-July/346690.html) but this is my first patch submission... we'll see.~~

## TODO

- [x] Import video files to OGV.
- [x] Imported video files working in the editor.
- [x] Imported video files working in project exports.
- [x] Add speed level option to this importer and FFmpeg.
- [x] Add a setting to disable the video importer when using an external addon for videos?
- [x] Show a warning when using an old FFmpeg version that doesn't support `-speed_level` and importing with a speed setting different to the FFmpeg default.

## Possible improvements?

- Imports can be really slow, as expected, and freeze the editor while importing. Can anything be done about this?
- Create asset library addon or automation to install FFmpeg? Calinou has proposed to create an external tools manager to centralize downloads.

summary: [Bug]: Android Export Encryption Issue in Godot 4.4.1

description.text:
### Description

When exporting to Android in Godot 4.4.1, resources cannot be encrypted into a PCK file unless APK extension is enabled. This limitation requires OBB files for additional resources, which complicates the export process. Users expect traditional export methods similar to Windows, where all resources are encrypted into a single PCK file that can be decrypted at runtime.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. Open Godot 4.4.1.
2. Attempt to export a project to Android with APK extension disabled.
3. Observe that resources are not encrypted into a PCK file.
4. Note that OBB files are generated for additional resources.

### Expected Behavior

The exported Android APK should include encrypted resources in a PCK file, without requiring APK extension or OBB files. The PCK file should be decrypted at runtime using a hardcoded key: `2B9E5A364378E535544AA88D1F379DA22EBD3AAD2B7ECDCDE84A8BE72CC9D317`.

### Additional Context

This feature is essential for simplifying the Android export process while maintaining security and compatibility with traditional Windows export methods.

summary: Class reference for AnimationPlayer doesn't specify that only some track types work in reverse

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.5.beta.custom_build.c6d130abd

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta (c6d130abd) - macOS Sequoia (15.5.0) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M4 Pro - Apple M4 Pro (14 threads) - 24.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Related to https://github.com/godotengine/godot-docs/issues/11108, but this is for the class reference instead of the manual. As explained there, I tried reversing an animation that used Animation Playback tracks, and found it didn't work correctly. A search through the Godot repository turned up https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/88518, suggesting that this is a known problem. In the long run, I think it'd be best to try to fix this, but for now, it would be good to at least have the documentation state upfront that not all track types will respect the speed scale.

[The class reference documentation for `AnimationPlayer.speed_scale`](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_animationplayer.html#class-animationplayer-property-speed-scale) simply states that the property changes the rate at which an animation plays. 
Similarly, [the documentation for `play()` and `play_backwards()`](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/classes/class_animationplayer.html#class-animationplayer-method-play) do not specify that some track types, such as Animation Playback tracks, will not play in reverse or at different speeds.

### Steps to reproduce

Create animations in an AnimationPlayer, then place them within Animation Playback tracks of another AnimationPlayer. When this AnimationPlayer's animation is played forwards with `play()` it will work fine; however, using `play_backwards()` the "sub-animations" will not play.

I've personally only had this happen with Animation Playback tracks, but I expect there would be similar behavior with other track types too, such as Audio Playback tracks. I should be able to do thorough testing in the future to determine exactly which track types support speed scales/playing backwards.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test-animation-speed_2025-07-16_14-41-44.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21266699/test-animation-speed_2025-07-16_14-41-44.zip)

summary: VisualShader: Add a VectorCoordinateTransform node

description.text: Partially supersedes: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/97215 Partially implements: https://github.com/godotengine/godot-proposals/issues/7666 ~~Depends on: https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108564~~ **I have opted for another not-so-radical solution that consists of creating a new built-in define, WORLD_VERTEX_COORDS, to detect if the world_vertex_coords flag is active.** This PR simply adds a new node to the visual shader editor that allows for transformation of a Vector3 from one coordinate space to another. From spaces: Model to..., World to..., View to..., Tangent to..., Screen to... To spaces: Model, World, View, Tangent, Screen Vector types: Position, Direction Normalize: True or False **All space conversions in vector position type:** **In addition to being able to change the position vector type to direction when you're in direction mode, you can normalize the result for improved performance, or not normalize it if you don't need to.** As you can see, this PR implements two new spaces, Tangent space and Screen space. These are based on the approach Unity took for its Transform Helper node. Therefore, the best way to test if this PR is working correctly is by using Unity. I've performed several comparisons with Unity, and it should mostly function the same, with the exception of a few cases. Unity flips the Y-axis in screen space, but Godot does not: | ScreenUV(Godot) | ScreenPosition(Unity) | |--------------|--------------| ||| Some transitions involving tangent space might show this result: This also used to happen in Unity, but it seems they've found a way to fix it: **Important Notes:** **I've replaced "clip space" with "screen space" as Unity does, since it returns a Vector3. However, it's possible that only the X and Y axes are necessary, and the Z-axis might be redundant, but I'm not certain.** **I'm also open to any suggestions that provide a better solution than what's currently implemented.** **Additionally, if someone could please test all possible combinations to see if I've made any errors in the conversions, that would be greatly appreciated.**

summary: Add check for existing bracket before automatically adding closing bracket

description.text:
Fixes #104548
Fixes #78983

When you add a new start bracket or quotation mark it checks whether it closed any existing brackets or strings on the current line. If it did then it avoids automatically adding an additional end bracket.

Before:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/adc3a873-a062-4ce9-b16e-bb58fa830925

After:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8eae7625-37df-4df2-aa45-9a6f6207be9c

More examples of the auto brace completion being aware of its surroundings:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0137e3d4-8324-4954-a11a-6601fe589871

For performance reasons it only checks for other brackets on the same line. It could however be extended to check multiple lines if that is desired. When creating brackets inside a string it only checks for other brackets inside that string instead of the whole line.
I have tested it with a bunch of different bracket combinations to make sure auto brace completion still works when it needs to. I have also tested it with multiple cursors.

summary: Editor ignores special keys when using alternative keyboard layout (Neo2, NeoQwertz)

description.text: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in v4.4.beta3.official [06acfccf8] and later (tested up to v4.5.beta3.official [4d1f26e1f]) - Non-reproducible in v4.4.beta2.official [a013481b0] and earlier (tested down to v3.6.1.stable.official [b1ba98fce]) ### System information Fedora Linux 42 (Workstation Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver - Gnome 48 ### Issue description I am using an alternative keyboard layout called NeoQwertz (https://www.neo-layout.org/Layouts/neoqwertz/) which adds additional layers to have more functions available close to the home row. The physical keyboard type is German ISO. Up to 4.4.beta2 this works just fine with Godot, but starting with 4.4.beta3 the special keys on layer 4 don't work anymore. When holding down a mod key the left half of the alpha keys become special keys like the arrow keys, backspace, delete, return, escape and tab (the right part basically becomes a numpad). See the following image on how the layout looks. Mod4 / M4 being the mod keys that activate the layer. The left half of layer 4 does not work anymore since 4.4.beta3. The right half of the same layer, containing mostly the number keys, however still works. Only non-printable chars / keys seem to be affected. At first I thought the mod keys were the problem, as I think both of them are specific to the ISO layout (the one to the right of shift simply does not exists in ANSI and the one to the right of space has a different keycode I assume), but that would not explain only the left half being affected. I have searched the issue tracker and found a number of issues related to keyboard layouts and physical keycodes vs "normal" keycodes but none quite fit and all of them were closed anyway. Though it made me suspect that the flexible, thus probably complicated, handling of shortcuts in Godot in relation to keycode vs physical location might be part of the problem. Maybe some fix introduced to handle other edge cases caused this? Looking for keyboard related merges between 4.4.beta2 and 4.4.beta3 is probably a good place to start. I want to stress that I have been using this layout for quite some time and I never had any problems with any desktop application. I assume that Godot is different because of the physical keycode functionality. Things I have already tested to narrow down the problem: - Tested the Colemak layout, which swaps capslock out for backspace. That works for all Godot versions I tested. The difference is that in this case the remapped key is on the first layer. Now thinking the problem only arises when a second key is held, I tried with NeoQwertz again but locked the layer 4 (by pressing both Mod4 keys at the same time the layer remains active until both of them are pressed again). It still did not work, so the second key being pressed at the same time does not seem to be the culprit. Maybe it has to do with the control keys being on a higher layer? - I used the key remapper kanata to remap the spacebar to backspace, which worked in all versions of Godot, supporting the previous point that just the remapping of a special key does not seem to be the main cause of the problem. - I tested with KDE in addition to Gnome, to rule out that the problem is Gnome specific (the application of the layout is done by the desktop environment). I do understand that this is a rather niche issue, but maybe someone familiar with the input system has an idea or can point me towards further tests to narrow down the issue. ### Steps to reproduce (I don't know if this is reproducible without an ISO keyboard. I don't know it the issue could theoretically be reproduced on Windows or Mac.) - change keyboard layout to NeoQwertz or Neo2 - both are the same on layer 3 and higher, but Neo2 also changes the alpha keys - both should be preinstalled on modern linux distributions - in Gnome only Neo2 is visible by default, to make NeoQwertz visible execute "gsettings set org.gnome.desktop.input-sources show-all-sources true" - layout names: German (Neo, QWERTZ) and German (Neo 2) - start Godot (4.4.beta3 or newer) and open any script file or editable text box - hold down one of the Mod4 keys ("Alt Gr" or "<", see picture of layout) and type for example: - F -> should behave like right arrow - V -> should behave like return - X -> should behave like tab - observe that nothing is happening ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A

summary: GPUParticles2D particles offscreen get rendered when Camera2D moves leaving large amounts of artifacts on the screen, freed particles are still rendered too.

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4.1

### System information

Win 11 - Nvidia RTX4070 - intel i5 13600kf - Godot Forward+

### Issue description

Check the video for the best understanding.

It seems that the particles when they are off screen still somehow get rendered and get "stuck" at the last moment and when the camera pans around they are all getting rendered at the same time creating this large trail of particles which shouldn't exist.
This happens **even after** the particle system was **freed** (way after), but also when the particle system is still around elsewhere in the world.

I don't see a way to deal with this through code, you can set the Visibility Rect to some massive value but then you are rendering tons and tons of particles which are off-screen which is why you would normally want to use the Visibility Rect as a tool to avoid exactly that, is that correct please?

Un/checking Interpolate makes no difference.

**Video**
_(in the video I early on said the particles are being rendered when the visibility rect is outside the camera, what I meant is that they shouldn't be getting rendered, I just mispoke, it does not change anything about the issue but I just wanted to clarify it here anyway.)_
https://youtu.be/nkpyMWJg9Lo (the red circles are the particles.)

### Steps to reproduce

Add a GPUParticle2D node, have it move past the camera, wait until it's far away and then pan the camera in that direction and observe the trail of particle effects as in the video.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[TestParticles.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/21204406/TestParticles.zip)

Video about how to use the MRP: https://youtu.be/3Bbr5F_QCpw

As mentioned in the description, you can make a ridiculously large visibility rect for the particles (size w/h: 10000, x/y: -5000) and observe that this issue doesn't occur (well until the visibility rect is way past the camera range obviously). But this would mean drawing all the particles outside the camera all the time which isn't a solution or even a decent workaround.

When using the Godot Android app with the Gboard keyboard, the gesture feature to slide the spacebar for moving the cursor (commonly used for precise cursor placement) doesn't work reliably. Create a new scene, make the root node a Path3D.
Add 2 points to the path and save the scene.

summary: Exported variant variable is deleted on script change

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.5 dev 5, 4.5 beta 3, v4.5.beta (715c58309)

### System information

Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.7602) - 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-12700F (20 threads) - 31.84 GiB memory

### Issue description

When a resource exports a variable as a variant and the variable is set and saved, it saves correctly to tres. If the script is changed and saved, the variable is reset to null in the editor. The tres file is unchanged, and reloading the project shows the correct value again. 

When a node exports a variable as a variant it is similar to what happens with a resource, except that when the script is changed the variable is deleted from the tscn file. Reloading the project will not fix it, the data is not there.

The same thing happens when the resource or scene are saved as res and scn.

### Steps to reproduce

(Resource)
Create a resource script that exports a variable of type variant
Create a resource of that type
Set the variable to anything
Change the script and save it. The code doesn't need to be changed, adding or removing a comment is enough.
The variable becomes null in the editor, not in the tres file.

(Node)
Create a script on a node exporting a variable of type variant
Set the variable to anything
Change the script and save it. The code doesn't need to be changed, adding or removing a comment is enough.
The variable becomes null in the editor and is deleted from the tscn file.

### System information

Godot v4.5.beta (4d1f26e1f) - Fedora Linux 42 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 575.64) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory

### Issue description

LightmapGI occasionally displays negative colors on dynamic objects. See this video where negative colors are highlighted as magenta:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3c2f96a5-37a4-45b7-80a5-f9e703dfdbd7

This was recorded with the Linear tonemapper, as certain tonemappers clamp negative colors and make the detection shader fail. This was also recording with debanding disabled, as it currently introduces slightly negative colors on certain black (but non-negative) pixels.

### Steps to reproduce

- Bake LightmapGI in a dark scene and only lights coming from emissive surfaces (so that dynamic objects don't receive any direct light, which makes the issue less noticeable).
- Place some objects with the **Dynamic** global illumination mode in the scene.
